title: Relationships Truth Stack
summary: Relationships are built from attachment, communication, trust, and repair, and the best sources explain those mechanics without turning them into slogans.
starterPack: Start with attachment, communication, and repair before reaching for personality labels.
Use research-backed relationship science before advice culture.
Let good books and trained clinicians outrank viral relationship takes.

tiers.title: First Principles
tiers.description: The core mechanics of healthy connection.

items.name: Attachment shapes closeness
items.description: People differ in how they seek security, autonomy, and reassurance.

items.name: Trust is built through consistency
items.description: Reliability matters more than intensity.

items.name: Repair matters after conflict
items.description: Healthy relationships are not conflict-free; they recover well.

items.name: Gottman Institute
items.description: Long-running relationship research and practical tools.

items.name: APA relationship research
items.description: Psychology research across attachment and intimacy.

items.name: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
items.description: Academic journal for relationship science.

items.name: John Gottman
items.description: Researcher known for studying conflict and repair.

items.name: Sue Johnson
items.description: Attachment-focused couples therapist and author.

items.name: Esther Perel
items.description: Popular voice on intimacy, desire, and connection.

tiers.title: Practitioners
tiers.description: People working directly with relationship dynamics.

items.name: Couples therapists
items.description: Help partners identify patterns and repair breakdowns.

items.name: Family therapists
items.description: Work with broader family systems and tensions.

items.name: Mediators
items.description: Support conflict resolution where interests differ.

items.name: Therapy clinics
items.description: Where couples and families seek structured help.

items.name: Family law systems
items.description: Where relationship breakdown becomes formal process.

items.name: Counselling associations
items.description: Set practice standards and training pathways.

items.name: Psychology Today
items.description: Accessible relationship and psychology writing.

items.name: Greater Good Magazine
items.description: Evidence-informed writing on connection and wellbeing.

items.name: The Gottman Blog
items.description: Practical research-backed relationship guidance.

items.name: Esther Perel Talks
items.description: Public talks on intimacy and connection.

items.name: TED Talks on relationships
items.description: Wide public discussions of intimacy and family life.

items.name: The School of Life
items.description: Philosophical relationship explainers.

tiers.title: Frontier Watchlist
tiers.description: What is changing around relationships now.

items.name: Digital dating norms
items.description: App design and swiping behaviour are reshaping mate choice.

items.name: Loneliness research
items.description: The public-health cost of weak social connection is increasingly studied.

items.name: Therapy literacy
items.description: More people are learning relationship language through popular psychology.
